text.title: Vision Applications — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every production VLM-task service must enforce: size cap, detail default, schema-in-prompt, normalize outputs, async batch cap, human-review threshold.

rule.statement: Validate image file size BEFORE base64 encoding. Reject > 20 MB (OpenAI cap) or > 5 MB (Anthropic cap) early. Reject unsupported formats (only jpeg / png / webp / gif allowed).

rationale.source: OpenAI 20 MB and Anthropic 5 MB API limits

rule.rationale: Base64 expands payload by ~33%; a 20 MB image becomes ~27 MB on the wire. Pre-validation prevents wasted API spend and confusing 400 errors.

rule.statement: Use `detail="auto"` as default; switch to `detail="high"` ONLY for dense text documents (invoice with small print, chart with labels). Forbid `detail="high"` on classification tasks.

rationale.source: OpenAI vision pricing: detail=high consumes 85 tokens per 512x512 tile + 85 base

rule.rationale: A 1024px image at detail=high costs ~525 tokens; at detail=low ~85 tokens. Misusing high on a yes/no classification quadruples cost with no accuracy gain.

rule.statement: Include the exact JSON schema in the prompt AND set `response_format={"type": "json_object"}`. Parse with try/except and re-prompt on JSONDecodeError or ValidationError.

rationale.source: json_object alone does not enforce schema; OpenAI Cookbook + production traces

rule.rationale: json_object guarantees valid JSON but not field names. Schema-in-prompt + retry-on-parse is the empirical sweet spot for schema adherence.

rule.statement: Normalise categorical output fields: severity / category / status MUST be lowercased before being persisted or routed. Reject responses where the field does not match the allowed enum after normalisation.

rationale.source: Severity drift incidents: HIGH vs High vs high broke downstream routing

rule.rationale: VLMs occasionally return "HIGH" or "Medium" instead of the expected enum value. Normalising at the boundary prevents silent downstream branching failures.

rule.statement: For batch processing, use `asyncio.gather` with a semaphore capped at 10-20 concurrent requests. Never run sequential batch with `for` loop in production.

rationale.source: Sequential vs parallel batch benchmark: 10-100x throughput delta

rule.rationale: Sequential batches at production volume hit pipeline-level latency budgets. The 10-20 cap balances throughput with provider tier-2 rate limits.

rule.statement: For content moderation, set `needs_human_review = true` whenever `confidence < 0.7` OR severity is `medium` / `high`. Never auto-action low-confidence moderation results.

rationale.source: False-negative incidents on subtly harmful content

rule.rationale: VLM moderation is probabilistic; the false-negative rate on subtle harm is non-zero. The threshold + review queue prevents policy-violation incidents reaching production.
